subject: Mathematics
q: If a = b, b = a, then a = c, this is called ___________
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on ASA
feedback: If a = b and b = c, then a=c, this is called "Transitive axiom". However, condition given is "if a = b and b = a", thus it is "None of above"

subject: Mathematics
q:
In triangle ABC, BE and AD are perpendicular to AC and BC respectively and intersect at point F, DG and EH are perpendicular to BF and AF respectively. Given \[ \angle ABC = 45^{\circ} ,\;\]
how many angles in this figure is equal to \[ \angle AFE\;\] other than itself?
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on ASA
feedback: Proving \[ \Delta BDF \cong \Delta ADC ,\;\] with right-angle triangles, we have \[ \angle AFE = \angle AEH = \angle BFD = \angle BDG = \angle C. \;\]

subject: Mathematics
q:
Given that \[ \angle A = \angle D, \angle B = \angle E ,\;\]
with which of the following conditions are triangle ABC and DEF congruent?
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on ASA
feedback:
Having \[ \angle A = \angle D,\;and\;\angle B = \angle E,\;\]
with the third condition in option D, BC = EF, the two triangles satisfy theorem 'AAS', hence triangles ABC and DEF are congruent to each other.

subject: Mathematics
q: If a triangle has two angles and one side opposite to one of these angles equal to those of another triangle, which of the following steps  derives from the theorem 'ASA' to corollary 'SAA' is incorrect?
options: From the two corresponding equal triangles, we know the third angles of the two triangles are also equal.
From the corresponding sides, we know that the other two sides are also equal correspondingly.
As we have three corresponding angles and one side, we can pick the two angles next to the side which are also equal correspondingly.
From the angles next to the side, with the side together, we can then prove the congruency of the two triangles by theorem 'ASA'.
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on ASA
feedback: The equality of the other two sides could be the outcome after proving the congruency of the two triangles, but not simply from the corresponding equal sides themselves.

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the following statements is the most appropriate one?
options: If we have two right triangles with any two sides equal, the two triangles are congruent to each other.
If we have two triangles with two equal corresponding angles and one of the sides equal, the two triangles are congruent to each other.
If we have two triangles with 2 equal corresponding angles and one of the sides between two corresponding equal angles to be equal, the two triangles are congruent to each other.
All of the above statements are correct.
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on ASA
feedback: All above statement are correct

subject: Mathematics
q: In a △ABC, AB = 7cm, AC = 4cm. If the perpendicular bisector of BC intersect AB and BC at D and E respectively, what is the perimeter of △ACD?
skill: Prove that in a triangle, sides opposite to congruent angles are congruent
feedback:
As DE is the perpendicular bisector to side BC, then we have BD = CD (where \[ \Delta BDE \cong \Delta CDE\;),\;\]
so the perimeter of △ACD = AD + AC + DC = AD + AC + BD = AC + AB = 11 cm.

subject: Mathematics
q: In a △ABC, AB = 5cm, AC = 3cm. If the perpendicular bisector of BC intersect AB and BC at D and E respectively, what is the perimeter of △ACD?
skill: Prove that in a triangle, sides opposite to congruent angles are congruent
feedback:
As DE is the perpendicular bisector to side BC, then we have BD = CD (where \[\Delta BDE \cong \Delta CDE\;),\;\]
so the perimeter of △ACD = AD + AC + DC = AD + AC + BD = AC + AB = 8 cm.

subject: Mathematics
q: Isosceles triangle ABC as shown in the figure, draw a line BD and join points A and D, line AD intersects side BC at E, F is a point on BC such that AE = AF. If AB = BD and AF // BD, how many angles are equal to ∠BAE excluding itself?
skill: Prove that in a triangle, sides opposite to congruent angles are congruent
feedback: Proving \[\Delta ABE \cong \Delta ACF\;,\;\] with AB = BD and AF // BD, we have ∠BAE = ∠D = ∠EAF = ∠CAF.

subject: Mathematics
q: The Isosceles triangle ABC is shown in the figure below. Imagine a point D which can be connected to point A so that AD bisects BC at point E. Connect D to B to form triangle DBE. F is a point on BC. Under which condition listed below would \[\Delta DBE \cong \Delta ACF\;?\;\]
options: When E and F is the same point
skill: Prove that in a triangle, sides opposite to congruent angles are congruent
feedback:
With the conditions, we have AE (AF) being the perpendicular bisector of triangle ABC, so \[ \angle AEB = \angle AEC = \angle DEB \;\]
which are all right angles. Since AB= BD, \[ \angle D = \angle BAE = \angle CAE.\;\]
Hence, we can prove that \[\Delta DBE \cong \Delta ACF \;\]

subject: Mathematics
q:
If triangle ABC has \[ \angle B = \angle C \;\]
which of the following statements is correct?
skill: Prove that in a triangle, sides opposite to congruent angles are congruent
feedback: By theorem 10.1.2, the opposite sides of ∠B and ∠C are AC and AB respectively, so AC = AB.

subject: Mathematics
q: Isosceles triangle COB, extend their sides CO and BO to A and D respectively with the same length. If we have AB = DC, which of the following is not correct?
options: If we connect A and D, AD//BC
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on SSS
feedback:
As AB = CD, BO = CO, AO = DO, triangles ABO and DCO are congruent, so ∠A = ∠D, ∠ABO = ∠DCO. Since ∠BOC = ∠AOD, AO = DO, if we connect AD, then
\[ \angle OAD = \frac{1}{2} (180^{\circ} - \angle DOA) = \frac{1}{2} (180^{\circ} - \angle BOC) = \angle OBC,\;\]
Hence lines AD and BC are parallel. However, no evidence can be found to prove that ∠ABC = ∠A.

subject: Mathematics
q: △ABC is isosceles. Given BD = CE, AD = AE, if ∠ABC = 70°, ∠DAE = ?
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on SSS
feedback:
By proving \[\Delta ABD \cong \Delta ACE \;\]
we have ∠BAC = ∠DAE. Then ∠DAE = ∠BAC = 180° - 70° x 2 = 40°.

subject: Mathematics
q: An isosceles triangle ABC as shown below, AE bisects BC and extends such that AE = DE, join B to D. If we want prove that \[\;\Delta BDE \cong \Delta CAE\;\] by theorem 'SSS', which of the following steps is not necessary?
options:
As triangle ABC is isosceles, AE bisects BC, so AE is perpendicular to BC as well. With \[ BE = BE, \: \angle BEA = \: \angle BED = 90^\circ \: , AE = DE,\;\]
△ABE and △DBE are congruent.
From \[ \Delta ABE\;and\;\Delta DBE\;\]  are congruent, we know that \[ \angle D = \angle EAC.\;\]

From \[ \Delta ABE\;and\;\Delta DBE\;\] are congruent, we know that DB = AB.
From isosceles triangle, we know AB = AC, thus, DB = AC.
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on SSS
feedback: Since we are using the three sides to prove the congruency of the two triangles, the equality of \[ \angle D\; and\;\angle EAC\;\] is not needed.

subject: Mathematics
q: If two triangles have all their three sides equal correspondingly, the two triangles are:
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on SSS
feedback: By theorem 'SSS', these two triangles are congruent. The right term for two 'equal' triangles is congruent.

subject: Mathematics
q: In an isosceles right-angle triangle ABC, ∠ABC = 90°. E is a point on CB. Extend AB to F and join C to F such that CF = AE. Which of the following conditions is not necessary for proving the congruency of triangles ABE and CBF?
options: Triangles ABE and CBF are right-angle triangles.
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on HL
feedback: With all above 3 conditions, we can prove the conclusion by theorem 'HS'.

subject: Mathematics
q: In an isosceles right-angle triangle ABC, ∠ABC = 90°. E is a point on CB. Extend AB to F and join C to F such that CF = AE. If ∠EAB = 10°, what is the measurement of ∠CFE?
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on HL
feedback: Having AB = BC, AE = CF, by theorem 'HS', right-angle triangles ABE and CBF are congruent. So ∠FCB = ∠EAB = 10°, ∠CFE = 90° - ∠FCB - ∠EFB = 90° - 10° - 45° = 35°.

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle ABC as seen below, line CD divides AB into two equal halves. \[DE \perp BC\] and \[\angle A = \angle ACD\]. If \[ \angle BDE = 49^\circ\], then find \[\angle ADC \].
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on HL
feedback: Having CD = AD = BD, DE = DE, right-angle triangles CDE and BDE are congruent (HS). So ∠BDE = ∠ CDE = 49°, ∠ ADC = 180° - ∠BDE - ∠CDE = 82°.

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle ABC as seen below, line CD divides AB into two equal halves. DE⊥BC and ∠A = ∠ACD. If ∠BDE = 56°, then ∠ADC = ?
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on HL
feedback: Having CD = AD = BD, DE = DE, right-angle triangles CDE and BDE are congruent (HS). So ∠BDE = ∠ CDE = 56°, ∠ ADC = 180° - ∠BDE - ∠CDE = 68°.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the two triangles ABC and ADE, C and E lie on lines AD and AB respectively, O is the intersection of BC and DE. ∠A is 90°, AC = AE and BE = CD. If ∠EOB = 40°, what is the measurement of ∠AED?
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on HL
feedback:
Since AE = AC, AD = AC + CD = AE + BE = AB, the right-angle triangles ABC and ADE are congruent (HS).
Then ∠D = ∠B
∠AED + ∠D = ∠AED + ∠B = 90° ......(1)
And, ∠B + ∠EOB = 180° - ∠BEO = ∠AED, \[ \Rightarrow \;\] ∠AED - ∠B = ∠EOB = 40° ......(2)
∠AED + ∠B = 90° ......(1)
∠AED - ∠B = 40° .......(2)
By Adding (1) & (2),we can calculate that ∠AED = 65°.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the two triangles ABC and ADE, C and E lie on lines AD and AB respectively. O is the intersection of BC and DE. ∠A is 90°, AC = AE and BE = CD. If ∠EOB = 18°, what is the measurement of ∠AED?
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on HL
feedback:
Since AE = AC, AD = AC + CD = AE + BE = AB, the right-angle triangles ABC and ADE are congruent (HS).

Then \[\angle D = \angle B\;,\; \angle AED + \angle D = \angle AED + \angle B = 90° (1).\;\]
And, \[\angle B + \angle EOB = 180° - \angle BEO = \angle AED, \Rightarrow \angle AED - \angle B = \angle EOB = 18° (2).\;\]

Putting (1) and (2) together, we can calculate that \[\angle AED = 54°.\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In the two triangles ABC and ADE, C and E lie on lines AD and AB respectively. ∠A is 90°, AC = AE and BE = CD. If ∠B = 32°, what is the measurement of ∠AED?
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on HL
feedback: Since AE = AC, AD = AC + CD = AE + BE = AB, the right-angle triangles ABC and ADE are congruent (HS). Then ∠D = ∠B = 25°, so ∠AED = 90° - 32° = 58°.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the two triangles ABC and ADE, C and E lie on lines AD and AB respectively. ∠A is 90°, AC = AE and BE = CD. If ∠B = 25°, what is the measurement of ∠AED?
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on HL
feedback: Since AE = AC, AD = AC + CD = AE + BE = AB, the right-angle triangles ABC and ADE are congruent (HS). Then ∠D = ∠B = 25°, so ∠AED = 90° - 25° = 65°.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the two triangles shown below AC = AB. Which of the following conditions is not enough to prove that triangles ADB and ADC are congruent?
options: when triangles ADB and ADC are right-angle triangles.
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on HL
feedback:
∠BAD = ∠CAD: SAS.
BD = CD: SSS.
Triangles ADB and ADC are right-angle triangles.: HS.
However, as ∠B and ∠C are not the included angle of the corresponding equal sides, we do not have a theorem to prove such a pair of triangles to be congruent.

subject: Mathematics
q: To prove two acute triangles to be congruent, which of the following theorems is not possible?
skill: Prove congruency of two triangles based on HL
feedback: HS is only for right-angle triangles but not acute ones, so HS is not possible here.

subject: Mathematics
q: The diagonals of a parallelogram _____________ each other
skill: Prove properties of parallelogram
feedback: The diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.

subject: Mathematics
q: The diagonals of a parallelogram ____________ each other
skill: Prove properties of parallelogram
feedback: The diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.

subject: Mathematics
q: In parallelogram ABCD, AD = 7, AC = 13, BD = 15. What is the perimeter of triangle BOC?
skill: Prove properties of parallelogram
feedback: The perimeter of \[ \Delta BOC = BC + OB + OC = AD +  \frac{1}{2}  (AC + BD) =7+ \frac{28}{2}=21.\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: How many pairs of congruent triangles are there in parallelogram ABCD shown below?
skill: Prove properties of parallelogram
feedback: The congruent triangles are: \[\Delta AOB \cong \Delta COD , \Delta BOC \cong \Delta AOD, \Delta ABC \cong \Delta ADC, \Delta BCD \cong \Delta BAD.\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the following statements about a parallelogram ABCD is correct?
options: Opposite sides AB and CD are equal.
Opposite sides AC and BD are equal.
skill: Prove properties of parallelogram
feedback: Theorem 11.1.1 states, opposite sides of a parallelogram are pair-wise equal. Also, as the opposite sides are parallel, we can prove the equality of ∠A and ∠C.

subject: Mathematics
q: In a triangle ABC, ∠ACB = 90°. D is the midpoint of BC, CE // AD, DE is perpendicular to BC. If AC = 3, CD = 4, what is the perimeter of the quadrilateral ACED?
skill: Prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram
feedback: By proving AC // DE, ACED is a parallelogram. In the right-angle triangle ACD, AC = 3, CD = 4, so AD = 5(Using Pythagoras Theorem). The perimeter of ACED = \[ (3 + 5) \times 2 = 16.\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the set of conditions listed below can prove a quadrilaterial to be a parallelogram?
options: Parallelism and Equality of one pair of opposite sides
Parallelism of two pairs of opposite sides
Equality of two pairs of opposite sides
skill: Prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram
feedback: All three set of conditions can help us prove a quadrileteral to be parallelogram.

subject: Mathematics
q: In parallelogram ABCD, E and F are points on AD and BC respectively. If BE = DF, ∠BED = 180° - ∠EDF, quadrilateral BEDF is a ___________?
skill: Prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram
feedback: With the help of theorem 11.1.2 in the text book. We can prove that ED = BF and BE = FD i.e. two opposite sides are congurent and parallel. Hence BEDF is a parallelogram.

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the following statement is correct?
options: Quadrilaterals with equal and parallel opposite sides are parallelograms.
Opposite sides of parallelograms are parallel and equal.
If two diagonals of a quadrilateral bisect each other, it is a parallelogram.
skill: Prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram
feedback:
Parallelogram is a quadrilateral with parallel sides equal. Acording to therom 11.1.1 in the textbook. The given options B and C are correct i.e. in a parallelogram
(i) Opposite sides are congurent
(ii) Diagonals bisect each other

Hence all the above given options are correct.

subject: Mathematics
q: Prove that the line segment, joining the midpoints of two sides of a triangle, is parallel to the third side and is equal to one half of its length.
skill: Prove triangle mid-point properties
feedback:
Given:
In \[\triangle ABC\], the midpoints of \[\overline{AB}\] and \[\overline{AC}\] are \[L\] and \[M\], respectively.

To prove:
\[\overline{LM} \parallel \overline{BC}\] and \[m\overline{LM}=\frac{1}{2}m\overline{BC}\]

Construction:
Join \[M\] to \[L\] and produce \[\overline{ML}\] to \[N\] such that \[\overline{ML} \cong \overline{LN}\].
Join \[N\] to \[B\], and in the figure, name the angles \[\angle 1\], \[\angle 2\], and \[\angle 3\] as shown.

Proof:
Statements Reasons
In \[\triangle BLN \leftrightarrow \triangle ALM\],
\[\overline{BL} \cong \overline{AL}\] given
\[\angle 2 \cong \angle 1\] vertical angles
\[\overline{NL} \cong \overline{ML}\] construction
\[\therefore \triangle BLN \cong \triangle ALM\] SAS postulate
\[\angle A \cong \angle 3 …\] (i) corresponding angles of congruent triangles
\[\overline{BN} \cong \overline{AM} …\] (ii) corresponding sides of congruent triangles
But \[\overline{BN} \parallel \overline{AM}\] from (i); alternate angles
\[\therefore \overline{BN} \parallel \overline{MC} …\] (iii) \[M\] is a point on \[\overline{AC}\].
\[\overline{AM} \cong \overline{MC} …\] (iv) given
\[\Rightarrow \overline{BN} \cong \overline{MC} …\] (v) from (ii) and (iv)
\[\therefore BCMN\] is a parallelogram. from (iii) and (iv)
\[\Rightarrow \overline{BC} \parallel \overline{NL}\] or \[\overline{BC} \parallel \overline{LM}\] opposite sides of the parallelogram
\[\overline{BC} \cong \overline{NM} …\] (vi) opposite sides of the parallelogram
\[m\overline{LM} = \frac{1}{2} m\overline{NM} …\] (vii) construction
Hence, \[m\overline{LM} = \frac{1}{2} m\overline{BC}\] from (vi) and (vii)

subject: Mathematics
q: Triangles ABC and A'BC have a common side BC. P, Q, R, S are the mid-points of sides AB, AC, A'C and A'B respectively. If SR = 5, find PQ.
skill: Prove triangle mid-point properties
feedback:
By theorem 11.1.3,

\[ SR = \frac{1}{2} BC = PQ = 5.\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Triangles ABC and A'BC have a common side BC. P, Q, R, S are the mid-points of sides AB, AC, A'C and A'B respectively. If SR = 3, find PQ.
skill: Prove triangle mid-point properties
feedback:
By theorem 11.1.3,

\[ SR = \frac{1}{2} BC = PQ = 3.\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In trapezoid ABCD, AD // BC, E is the mid-point of CD. If BC = AD + 2CF, EF = 7, then find AB.
skill: Prove triangle mid-point properties
feedback:
Let's draw a auxiliary line as the figure below, DG // AB intersecting BC at point G. Then ABGD is a parallelogram, BG = AD.
\[ So \: CG = 2CF, \: with \: CD = 2CE, EF = \frac{1}{2} DG = \frac{1}{2}AB.\;\]
Hence, AB = 2EF = 14.

subject: Mathematics
q: In a triangle ABC with a line joining the mid-points of two sides, D & E, if BC = 10, find DE.
skill: Prove triangle mid-point properties
feedback:
By theorem 11.1.3

The Line segment joining the midpionts of two sides of a triangle, is parallel to the third side and is equal to one half of its length.

\[DE = \frac{1}{2} BC = 5\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In a triangle ABC with a line joining the mid-points of two sides, D & E, if BC = 5, then find DE.
skill: Prove triangle mid-point properties
feedback:
By theorem 11.1.3

The Line segment joining the midpionts of two sides of a triangle, is parallel to the third side and is equal to one half of its length.

\[DE = \frac{1}{2} BC = 2.5\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: The medians of a triangle intersect each other in the ratio of ___________
skill: Prove triangle median properties
feedback: Medians of a triangle intersect each other in the ratio of 1:2.

subject: Mathematics
q: In a parallelogram as shown in the figure below, AC and BD are the diagonals. E and F are the mid-points of BC and AD respectively. If DQ = 5, OQ = 2, then find BP.
skill: Prove triangle median properties
feedback: DE and CO are medians of △BCD, so CO = 3OQ. Similarly, AO = 3PO, as AO = CO, PO = OQ, with BO = OD, ∠BOP = ∠DOQ, triangles BOP and DOQ are congruent. So BP = QD = 5.

subject: Mathematics
q: In a parallelogram as shown in the figure below, AC and BD are the diagonals. E and F are the mid-points of BC and AD respectively. If OQ = 2, then find PO.
skill: Prove triangle median properties
feedback: DE and CO are medians of △BCD, so CO = 3OQ = 6. Similarly, AO = 3PO, as AO = CO, PO = 2.

subject: Mathematics
q: The median of a triangle are concurrent and this point of concurrency is the ____________ of each median.
skill: Prove triangle median properties
feedback: Refer to the proof of theorem 11.1.4, the point trisect all medians.

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle ABC, E and F are mid-points of line AC and AF, connect A and O intersecting BC at D. Extend BC to G so that CG = AC. If \[\; DC = \frac{1}{3} DG\;,\; BC = 12,\] then find \[AF \].
skill: Prove triangle median properties

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle ABC, E and F are mid-points of line AC and AB, connect A and O intersecting BC at D. Extend BC to G so that CG = AC. If \[\;DC = \frac{1}{3} DG\;,\; BC = 4\] then find \[AF\].
skill: Prove triangle median properties

subject: Mathematics
q: A triangle ABC, AD, BE and CF are the medians, intersecting at O. If DO = 5, find AD.
skill: Prove triangle median properties
feedback: By theorem 11.1.4, point O trisect the medians. So AD = 3DO = 15.

subject: Mathematics
q: A triangle ABC, AD, BE and CF are the medians, intersecting at O. If DO = 2, find AD.
skill: Prove triangle median properties
feedback: By theorem 11.1.4, point O trisect the medians. So AD = 3DO = 6.

subject: Mathematics
q: Two lines, AB and CD, are cut by a group of parallel lines. If \[\; DF = \frac{1}{2} DC\;,\; AB = 5\;,\;then\; AE =\; ?\;\]
skill: Prove parallel line properties
feedback:
By theorem 11.1.5 in the textbook,
\[ AE = \frac{1}{2} AB = 2.5.\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Two lines, AB and CD, are cut by a group of parallel lines. If AE = EB, CD = 3, then DF = ?
skill: Prove parallel line properties
feedback:
By theorem 11.1.5 in the textbook,
\[ DF = FC \Rightarrow DF = \frac{1}{2} CD = 1.5. \;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In trapezoid ABCD, E is a point trisects AB and EF // AD. If BC = 2EF, DF = 2.5, AD = 4, then what is the length of EF?
skill: Prove parallel line properties
feedback:
Let's extend BA and CD, intersecting at point G. BC = 2EF, and EF // AD, so E, F are mid-points of GB and GC ( in △GBC ).
\[ AE = \frac{1}{3} AB = \frac{1}{2} EB = \frac{1}{2} EG, \;\]
so GA = AE, with AD // EF, A and D are mid-points of GE and GF ( in △GEF ),
\[ AD = \frac{1}{2} EF. \Rightarrow EF = 2 \times AD = 8. \;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In trapezoid ABCD, E is a point trisects AB and EF // AD. If BC = 2EF, DF = 1.5, AD = 2, then what is the length of DC ?
skill: Prove parallel line properties
feedback:
Let's extend BA and CD, intersecting at point G. BC = 2EF, and EF // AD, so E, F are mid-points of GB and GC ( in △GBC ).
\[ AE = \frac{1}{3} AB = \frac{1}{2} EB = \frac{1}{2} EG,\;\]
so GA = AE, with AD // EF, A and D are mid-points of GE and GF ( in △GEF ),
\[ AD = \frac{1}{2} EF. Thus, DF = \frac{1}{3} DC\;\]
\[ DC = 3 \times DF = 4.5\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle ABC, D and E are mid-points of BC and AC. CO intersects AB at F and AG // CF. If EG = 7, then find BG.
skill: Prove parallel line properties
feedback: F is the mid-point of AB, FO // AG, so O is the mid-point of BG ( in △ABG ), so BO = OG, and BO = 2OE, \[ \Rightarrow \;\] BG = 4EG = 28.

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle ABC, D and E are mid-points of BC and AC. CO intersects AB at F and AG // CF. If EG = 2, then find BO.
skill: Prove parallel line properties
feedback: F is the mid-point of AB, FO // AG, so O is the mid-point of BG ( in △ABG ), so BO = OG, and BO = 2OE, so OE = EG = 2, \[ \Rightarrow\;\]  BO = 2OE = 4.

subject: Mathematics
q: A line that bisects the sides of a triangle at a right angle is called _____.
skill: Properties of right bisector of a line segment
feedback:
A line that bisects the sides of a triangle at a perpendicular angle is called a perpendicular bisector.
A median is the middle number in a sorted list of numbers, whereas a bisector also divides a line into two equal parts but not necessarily at a right angle.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the triangle \[ABC\], \[AD\] is the angle bisector of \[\angle BAC\]. The right bisector of line \[AD\] intersects \[AB\] and the extension of \[BC\] at points \[F\] and \[E\] respectively. If m\[\angle EAD = 56 ^o\] and m\[\angle EAC = 36 ^o\], then find m\[\angle B\].
skill: Properties of right bisector of a line segment
feedback:
Since \[EF\] is the right bisector of \[AD\], so

\begin{align}ED &= EA\\\therefore \angle EDA &= \angle EAD\\AF &= FD\\\angle FDA &= \angle FAD = \angle DAC\\\end{align}

\begin{align}\mbox{As } \angle ADE &= \angle B + \angle BAD\\\mbox{So } \angle B &= \angle ADE - \angle BAD\\&=\angle DAE - \angle DAC \\&= \angle EAC\end{align}

As \[\angle EAC= 36^o\] and \[\angle B = \angle EAC\] so, \[\angle B = 36^o\].

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle \[ABC\], \[DE \perp BC\]. If \[BD = CD\] and \[BE = 4\], then find \[BC\].
skill: Properties of right bisector of a line segment
feedback:
As \[BD = CD\], \[DE\] is the right bisector of \[BC\].

\begin{align}BE &=4\\BE & = CE\\BC &= BE + CE\\BC &= 2BE \\&= 2 \times 4\\& = 8\end{align}

Therefore, \[BC=8\].

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle \[ABC\], \[DE \perp BC\]. If \[BD = CD\] and \[BE = 2\], then find \[BC\].
skill: Properties of right bisector of a line segment
feedback:
As \[BD = CD\], \[DE\] is the right bisector of \[BC\].

\begin{align}BE &=2\\BE & = CE\\BC &= BE + CE\\BC &= 2 + 2 \\&= 4.\end{align}

Therefore, \[BC=4\].

subject: Mathematics
q: If two lines intersect each other, then the non-adjacent angles are called _____.
skill: Proof of any point that lies on the right bisector of a line segment
feedback:
If two lines intersect each other, then the non-adjacent angles are called vertical angles.

Angles that add up to \[90^\circ\] are called complementary angles and two angles that add up to \[180^\circ\] are called supplementary angels.

subject: Mathematics
q: The right bisector of a line segment _____.
options: has a length that is equal to the line segment
is half the length of the line segment
skill: Proof of any point that lies on the right bisector of a line segment
feedback:
The right bisector of a line segment is always unique.

The right bisector of a line segment cuts it into two equal parts at \[90^\circ\]. The bisector can be of any length.

subject: Mathematics
q: Which ONE of the following statements is correct regarding the points on a right bisector of a line segment?
options: Any point equidistant from any two points on a line segment is on the right bisector of the line segment.
Any point equidistant from the end points of a line segment is on the right bisector of the line segment.
Any point equidistant from the mid-point and an end point of a line segment is on the right bisector of the line segment.
Any point on the right bisector of a line segment is equidistant from the end points of the bisector.
skill: Proof of any point that lies on the right bisector of a line segment
feedback: The correct response is: Any point equidistant from the end points of a line segment is on the right bisector of the line segment.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the right-angle triangle \[ABC\], \[∠ACB = 90^\circ\]. If \[AE = EC\] and if \[D\] is the mid-point of \[AC\], then which of the following statements is NOT necessarily correct?
skill: Proof of any point that lies on the right bisector of a line segment
feedback:
In the given image, we can see that \[\triangle ACE\] is an isosceles triangle. As \[CD = AD\], and \[DE\] is the right bisector of \[AC\], we can confirm that \[ED // BC\] and \[ED \perp AC\].

However, \[EC\] does not necessarily bisect \[∠ ACB\], hence, the statement \[∠ACE = ∠BCE\] is not necessarily correct.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the quadrilateral below, if \[AC = AD\] and \[BC = BD\], then, _____.
options: \[CD\] and \[AB\] bisect each other at a right angle
\[CD\] and \[AB\] bisect each other at an acute angle
skill: Proof of any point that lies on the right bisector of a line segment
feedback:
We will prove that one of these line segments bisects the other by making a correspondence between \[ \triangle ABC\] and \[\triangle ABD\].
\[AC = AD\], \[BC = BD\], and \[AB\] is the common side (is equal to itself), so, as per the SSS postulate, \[ \triangle ABC \cong \triangle ABD\].
As a result, \[ \angle CAO = \angle DAO\] as they are the corresponding angles of congruent triangles.

Now that two sides and an angle of \[\triangle ACO\] are equal to the corresponding two sides and the angle, we can use the SAS postulate to state that \[\triangle ACO \cong \triangle ADO\].
Therefore, \[CO = DO\].

We already know that \[AC = AD\], and that \[ AB \perp CD.\]
Thus, \[AB\] is the right bisector of \[CD\].

Note that \[CD\] is NOT the right bisector of \[AB\] as it does not cut \[AB\] in half. In other words, \[AO \ne OB\].

subject: Mathematics
q: In right-angle triangle ABC, AC⊥BC. E is the point where the right bisectors of three sides of the triangle intersect. If the area of triangle CBE = 3.5, what would be the area of triangle ABC?
skill: Prove that the right bisectors of the sides of a triangle are concurrent
feedback: If we draw the right bisector of AC, then the 4 small triangles inside triangle ABC are congruent. As triangle BEC is the sum of two small triangles, the area of ABC would be twice of BEC.

subject: Mathematics
q: In right-angle triangle ABC, AC⊥BC. D is the point where the right bisectors of three sides of the triangle intersect. Draw DE⊥AC at E, if AE = 2, DE = 4, what is the area of triangle ABC?
skill: Prove that the right bisectors of the sides of a triangle are concurrent
feedback: DE is the right bisector of AC, so AE = EC, BC = 2DE. Area of triangle ABC = \[ \frac{1}{2} AC \times BC = \frac{1}{2} (2AE \times 2DE) = \frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 8 = 16 \;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In Right-angle triangle ABC, ∠ACB = 90°. ED is the right bisector of side AB, intersecting the extension of BC at point F. Given C is the mid-point of BF, FC = 3, what would be the perimeter of triangle ABF if we connect AF?
skill: Prove that the right bisectors of the sides of a triangle are concurrent
feedback: FE = EB = AE, ∠F = ∠EBF, ∠A = ∠ABE. As ∠A + ∠ABC = ∠F + ∠ABC = 90°, ∠F = ∠EBF = ∠A = ∠ABE. From ∠A + ∠ABE + ∠EBC = 90° we get ∠ABC = 60°. So triangle ABF is equilateral, the perimeter = FC * 6 = 18.

subject: Mathematics
q: Right-angle triangle ABC, where ∠ACB = 90°. FD is the right bisector of side AB, intersecting the extension of BC at point F. If C is the mid-point of BF, what would be the degree of ∠A?
skill: Prove that the right bisectors of the sides of a triangle are concurrent
feedback:
Since ∠F = ∠A AND ∠B = ∠A

Hence,

∠F = ∠B
∠F = ∠FBE + ∠EBA

As , ∠A + ∠B = 90°
So we get :

∠A + ∠FBE + ∠EBA = 90°
∠A + ∠F + ∠EBA = 90°
∠A + ∠A + ∠A = 90°
3 ∠A = 90°
∠A = 30°

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle \[ABC\], \[BO\] and \[CO\] are the angle bisectors of \[∠ ABC\] and \[∠ ACB\]. Furthermore, \[OD // AB\] and \[OE // AC\]. If \[AB = 12\], \[AC = 18\], and \[BC = 9\], what would be the perimeter of triangle \[ODE\]?
skill: Prove that any point on the bisector of an angle is equidistant from its arms
feedback:
\[∠ ABO = ∠ OBC = ∠ BOD\], \[\therefore BD = OD\].

Similarly, \[OE = EC\],so the perimeter of triangle \[ODE = BD + DE + EC = 9\].

subject: Mathematics
q: In the figure below, \[D\] is a point on the angle bisector of \[∠ ABC\]. Which of the following conditions will make the statement \[BP = BP'\] true for this image?
skill: Prove that any point on the bisector of an angle is equidistant from its arms
feedback: If \[PD = P'D\], then we have two equal distances from the angle bisector to the arms. By using this information, we can prove that \[\triangle BDP \cong \triangle BDP'\]. Hence, \[BP = BP'\] as they are the corresponding sides of congruent triangles.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the figure below, OB⊥CB, OA⊥AC, BO = AO, ∠BCA = 70° and O' is a point on C. If OE = OD, then find ∠OO'E.
skill: Prove that any point inside an angle, equidistant from its arms, is on the bisector of it
feedback: With the conditions given above, we can find ∠O'OE = 55.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the figure below, OB⊥CB, OA⊥AC, BO = AO and O' is a point on CO. If we want to prove ∠EO'O = ∠DO'O, which of the following condition is not sufficient if we add it on?
skill: Prove that any point inside an angle, equidistant from its arms, is on the bisector of it
feedback:
Firstly we can prove that CO is the angle bisector of ∠BCA.
With O'E // CB, O'D // CA: we have ∠EO'O = ∠BCO = ∠ACO = ∠DO'O.
With O'E⊥OB, O'D⊥OA: use 'AAS' to prove the congruency of triangles EO'O and DO'O.
With OE= OD: use 'SAS' to prove the congruency of triangles EO'O and DO'O.
With O'E = O'D, we are not able to prove the desired ∠EO'O = ∠DO'O

subject: Mathematics
q: In the proof of theorem 'any point inside an angle, equidistant from its arms, is on the bisector of it', using the following figure as an example, which of the following steps is not necessary?
options: BO is a common side of two triangles
skill: Prove that any point inside an angle, equidistant from its arms, is on the bisector of it
feedback: With options except the second, the congruency between triangles BOE and BOF can be proven, but before we arrive  at △BOE ≌ △BOF, we cannot state the equality of sides AE and FC yet.

subject: Mathematics
q: In a right-angle triangle ABC, ∠C = 90°, DE⊥AB. Given DE = DC, if BC = 8 and AC = 6, what would be the perimeter of △DBE?
skill: Prove that any point inside an angle, equidistant from its arms, is on the bisector of it
feedback:
From the given conditions, we have DE = CD, AE = AC,
Perimeter required = DB + DE + BE.
In right triangle ABC, since BC = 8 and AC = 6, we can find that AB= 10 (by Pythagorean Theorem).
AE = AC = 6, thus BE = 10 - 6 = 4
CD = DE , thus BD + DE = BD + CD = BC.
Thus, perimeter = BD + DE + BE
= BC + BE
= 8 + 4 = 12

subject: Mathematics
q: In right-angle triangle ABC, ∠C = 90°, DE⊥AB. Given DE = DC, AB = 10 and BC = 6, what would be the perimeter of △DBE?
skill: Prove that any point inside an angle, equidistant from its arms, is on the bisector of it
feedback:
From the given conditions, we have DE = CD, AE = AC,
Perimeter required = DB + DE + BE.
In right triangle ABC, since AB = 10 and BC = 6, we can find that AC= 8 (by Pythagorean Theorem).
AE = AC = 8, thus BE = 10 - 8 = 2.
CD = DE , thus BD + DE = BD + CD = BC.
Thus, perimeter = BD + DE + BE
= BC + BE
= 6 + 2 = 8

subject: Mathematics
q: In right-angle triangle ABC, ∠C = 90°, DE⊥AB. Given DE = DC, ∠B = 42°, then ∠CAD = ?
skill: Prove that any point inside an angle, equidistant from its arms, is on the bisector of it
feedback:
As we can prove that AD is the angle bisector of ∠BAC,
\[∠CAD = \frac{1}{2} ∠BAC = \frac{1}{2} (90° - ∠B) =\frac{1}{2} (90° - 42) =24°\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle ABN, C is a point on AN such that ∠NBC = ∠CBM and where M is the point where angle bisectors of triangle ABC intersect. Given ∠N = 32° and ∠ABC = 22°, then ∠ACB = ?
skill: Prove that the bisectors of the angles of a triangle are concurrent
feedback: ∠ABM = \[\frac{1}{2}\;\] ∠ABC = 11°. ∠ABM = ∠MBC = ∠CBN = 11°, ∠ACB = ∠N + ∠CBN = 43°.  (Triangle external angle equal to the sum of non-adjacent internal angles)

feedback: ∠ABM = ∠MBC = ∠CBN = 18°, ∠ACB = ∠N + ∠CBN = 46°. (Triangle external angle equal to the sum of non-adjacent internal angles)

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle ABC, M is the concurrent point of the angle bisectors of the triangle. N and P are points on side BC such that CN = AC and BP = AB. If ∠B = 45° and ∠C = 60°, then what are the degrees of ∠NMP and ∠BAM?
skill: Prove that the bisectors of the angles of a triangle are concurrent
feedback:
AM bisects ∠BAC, thus ∠BAC = 2∠BAM, Thus, ∠BAM = \[\frac{ (180^{\circ} - 45^{\circ} - 60^{\circ})}{2} = 37.5^{\circ} \;\]

If we join up CM, we can prove△ACM ≌ △NCM, thus ∠MNP = ∠MAC, and ∠MAC= \[ \frac{1}{2} \;\] ∠BAC; thus, ∠BAC = 2∠MNP

Similarly, we can prove ∠MPN = ∠BAM. Thus, ∠NMP = 180° - (∠MNP + ∠MPN) = 180° - ∠BAC, finally, ∠NMP = ∠B + ∠C

Thus, ∠NMP = 45° + 60° = 105°

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle ABC, M is the concurrent point of the angle bisectors of the triangle (incenter). N and P are points on side BC such that CN = AC and BP = AB. Which of the following statements is not correct?
skill: Prove that the bisectors of the angles of a triangle are concurrent
feedback:
AM bisects ∠BAC, thus ∠BAC = 2∠BAM

If we join up CM, we can prove△ACM ≌ △NCM, thus ∠MNP = ∠MAC, and ∠MAC= \[\frac{1}{2}\;\] ∠BAC; thus, ∠BAC = 2∠MNP

Similarly, we can prove ∠MPN = ∠BAM. Thus, ∠NMP = 180° - (∠MNP + ∠MPN) = 180° - ∠BAC, finally, ∠NMP = ∠B + ∠C

subject: Mathematics
q: In triangle ABC, AM and BM are the angle bisectors of ∠BAC and ∠ABC respectively. Join M to C. Given that ∠BAC = 72° and ∠ABC = 40°, then ∠BMC = ?
skill: Prove that the bisectors of the angles of a triangle are concurrent
feedback:
As the three angle bisector are always concurrent, CM also bisects ∠ACB,
Thus ∠ACB = 180° - 72° - 40° = 68°.
And ∠BMC = 180° - ∠MBC - ∠MCB = 180° - 20° - 34° = 126°.

q: In a triangle, where will the longest side of a triangle be located?

skill: Prove that in a triangle, the longer side has an angle of greater measure opposite to it
feedback: The longest side of a triangle will be located across from the largest angle of the triangle.

subject: Mathematics
q: List the sides in \[\triangle ABC\] from the smallest to the largest.
topic: Sides and angles of a triangle
skill: Prove that in a triangle, the longer side has an angle of greater measure opposite to it
feedback: \[m\angle B = 180^\circ -65^\circ -40^\circ = 75^\circ\]. Hence, \[m\overline{AB} < m\overline{BC} < m\overline{AC}\] because angles opposite to these sides are \[40^\circ<65^\circ<75^\circ\] and the side opposite to the largest angle is the longest.

subject: Mathematics
q: Find the shortest side from the following figure.
topic: Sides and angles of a triangle
skill: Prove that in a triangle, the longer side has an angle of greater measure opposite to it
feedback:
\begin{align}m\angle QPR &= 180^\circ - 120^\circ\\m\angle QPR &= 60^\circ\\\\m\angle PRQ &= 180^\circ-140^\circ\\m\angle PRQ &= 40^\circ\\\\m\angle PQR&= 180^\circ-60^\circ-40^\circ\\m\angle PQR&= 80^\circ\end{align}

The shortest side will be the side opposite to \[\angle PRQ\]. Hence, \[PQ\] is the shortest side.

q:
ABC is an isosceles triangle with base BC. On an extension of BC point D lies so that BC < BD. A line segment through D cuts AC at L and AB at M. To prove that mAL>mAM,


We have the following steps:
1. m∠B=m∠ACB
2. m∠ACB>m∠CLD
3. m∠AML>m∠ACB
4. m∠AML>m∠B
5. But m∠ALM=m∠CLD, hence m∠AML>m∠ALM
6. m∠AML>m∠CLD Therefore mAL>mAM.
​Rearrange the steps in the correct order.
topic: Sides and angles of a triangle
skill: Prove that in a triangle, the side opposite to the greater angle is longer than the other sides
feedback: Prove m∠ACD > m∠CLD and m∠AMD>m∠B respectively. Therefore m∠AML > m∠ALM, by "If two angles of a triangle are unequal in measure, the side opposite to the greater angle is longer than the side opposite to the smaller angle", mAL>mAM.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the given triangle, which of the following values is NOT possible for \[x\]?
topic: Sides and angles of a triangle
skill: Prove that the sum of the lengths of any two sides in a triangle is greater than the third
feedback:
In a triangle, the sum of the lengths of any two sides is always greater than that of the third side.

\[x+1\]
\[6+1=7 \;\;\;\;\; 7+3=10\]
\[4+1=5 \;\;\;\;\; 5+3=8\]
\[5+1=6 \;\;\;\;\; 6+3=9\]
\[1+1=2 \;\;\;\;\; 2+3=5\]

All of these values of \[x\] result in the sum of the lengths of two sides being greater than the third side \[6\], except for \[x=1\]. Hence, \[x=1\] is not possible for the given triangle.

q: In a triangle ABC, AC = 5, if D is the mid-point of BC and AD = 7, then the range of the length of AB would be:
topic: Sides and angles of a triangle
skill: Prove that the sum of the lengths of any two sides in a triangle is greater than the third
feedback: 7 - 5 < DC < 7 + 5, which is 2 < DC < 12, so 2 < BD < 12. as AD > AC, ∠ADC must be acute, then ∠ADB is obtuse, which means its opposite side AB > AD or BD. So AD and BD are both shorter than AB, AB < AD + BD. Then 2 + 7 < AB < 12 + 7, which is 9 < AB < 19.

q:
Given △ABC~△DEF,i.e.,m∠A=m∠D,m∠B=m∠E and m∠C=m∠F. To prove mAB:mDE = mAC:mDF = mBC:mEF,
we have the following steps:


1. As shown above on AB take a point L, such that mAL=mDE. On AC take a point M such that mAM=mDF. Join L and M by the segment LM.
2.In △ALM←→△DEF,m∠A=m∠D mAL=mDE mAM=mDF, thus △ALM≡△DEF and m∠L=m∠E, m∠M=m∠F
3.Now m∠E=m∠B and m∠F=m∠C, thus m∠L=m∠B, m∠M=m∠C and LM||BC
4.mAB:mAL=mAM:mAC or mDE:mAB=mDF:mAC
5.Similarly by intercepting segments on BA and BC, we can prove that mDE:mAB=mEF:mBC

Hence mAB:mDE = mAC:mDF = mBC:mEF.
Which step above is wrong?
skill: Prove that if two triangles are similar, the length of their corresponding sides are proportional
feedback:
By Theorem 14.1.1, a line parallel to one side of a triangle divides the other two sides proportionally.

Hence mAL:mAB=mAM:mAC or mDE:mAB=mDF:mAC.

subject: Mathematics
q: Given △ABC with ED || CB as shown in the diagram below, if mDE=9, mBC=15 and mAE =6, What is the value of mAC?

feedback:
Since ED || CB, △ABC and △ADE are similar triangles. If two triangles are similar, the measures of their corresponding sides are proportional.
\[ mAC = 6 \times \frac{15}{9} = 10.\;\]

q: A right-angle triangle has two sides with lengths of 6 and 8 and another right-angle triangle, that is similar to it, has three sides of 3, 4 and x. How many sides could x possibly be?
skill: Prove that if two triangles are similar, the length of their corresponding sides are proportional
feedback:
If two triangles are similar, then the measures of their corresponding sides are proportional.

In a right-angle triangle, x can be a right-angle side or the hypotenuse.

Thus, x can be two possible values.

subject: Mathematics
q: What is the measure of the hypotenuse to the nearest tenth of a metre?
skill: Prove Pythagoras theorem for right angled triangle
feedback:
The height of this triangle is equal to the length of the square attached to it. We will find the length of the square from the given area first.

\begin{align}\mbox{Length of the square}&= \sqrt{\mbox{Area}}\\&= \sqrt{256}\\&= 16 \mbox{ m}\\\\\mbox{Hyp}^2&=\mbox{Perp}^2+ \mbox{Base}^2 \;\;\;\;\; (\because \mbox{Pythagoras' Theorem})\\\mbox{Hyp}^2&=16^2+ 32^2 \\\mbox{Hyp}^2&=256+ 1{,}024 \\\mbox{Hyp}^2&=1{,}280 \\\mbox{Hyp}&= 35.8 \mbox{ m}\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: What is the height of the rectangle?
skill: Prove Pythagoras theorem for right angled triangle
feedback: \begin{align}\mbox{Hyp}^2&=\mbox{Perp}^2+\mbox{Base}^2 \;\;\;\;\; (\because \mbox{Pythagoras' theorem}) \\\mbox{Perp}^2&=\mbox{Hyp}^2-\mbox{Base}^2 \\\mbox{Perp}^2&= 8^2-7^2\\\mbox{Perp}^2&=64-49\\\mbox{Perp}^2&=15\\\mbox{Perp}&=\sqrt{15}\mbox{ units}\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: If the square \[ABCD\], has an area of \[\frac{2}{9}\], what is the length of \[AC\]?
skill: Prove Pythagoras theorem for right angled triangle
feedback:
The area of the square \[ABCD = AD*DC\], so the sides of the square are \[ \frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}. \;\]

According to the Pythagoras' theorem, in the right-angled triangle \[ADC\] \[ AC^2 = AD^2 + DC^2 \].

\[ AC = \sqrt{AD^2 + DC^2}\]

\[ AC = \sqrt{\left( \frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}\right) ^2 + \left( \frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}\right) ^2}\]

\[ AC = \sqrt{\frac{4}{9}}\]

\[AC= \frac{2}{3}\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In the isosceles triangle \[ABC\], \[AD = 12\] and \[BC = BD = 9\]. If \[AD\] is perpendicular to \[BC\], find \[AB\].
skill: Prove Pythagoras theorem for right angled triangle
feedback:
In the right-angled triangle \[\triangle ABD\],
\begin{align}AB^2 &= AD\,^2 + BD\,^2 \mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{ }\because\mbox{Pythagoras' Theorem}\\AB^2 &= 12^2 + 9^2\\AB^2 &= 144+81 \\&=225\\\\AB &= \sqrt{225}\\AB&=15\end{align}

Therefore, \[AB = 15\].

subject: Mathematics
q: In the isosceles triangle \[ABC\], \[AD = 24\] and \[BC = BD = 7\]. If \[AD\] is perpendicular to \[BC\], find \[AB\].
skill: Prove Pythagoras theorem for right angled triangle
feedback:
In the right-angled triangle \[\triangle ABD\],
\begin{align}AB^2 &= AD\,^2 + BD\,^2 \mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{ }\because\mbox{Pythagoras' Theorem}\\AB^2 &= 24^2 + 7^2\\AB^2 &= 576+49 \\&=625\\\\AB &= \sqrt{625}\\AB&=25\end{align}

Therefore, \[AB = 25\].

q: The two sides of a right-angle triangle are 5 and 12. The third side has an odd length and the sum of the three sides is a multiple of 3. The third side must have a length of _______.
skill: Prove a triangle is right angled triangle bassed on reverse of Pythagoras theorem
feedback:
The third side can be the right-angle side or the hypotenuse of the right angled triangle.

​If it is the hypotenuse: \[\sqrt{5\,^2 + 12\,^2}= 13.\;\]

Check: 13+5+12 = 30 = multiple of 3.

If it is a right-angle side, \[\sqrt{12^2 - 5^2}= \sqrt{119}\;\] which does not meet the requirement.

​
The area of this triangle = \[ \frac{1}{2}\times 7\times24 \;=\;84\;\].

subject: Mathematics
q: Evaluate the values of \[x\] and \[y\].
skill: Parallelograms and their Properties
feedback:
To evaluate the values of \[x\] and \[y\] we need to consider the following property of parallelograms:
The diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other. Using this property, we can form the following \[2\] equations:

\[2y = 9\] ------- (i)
\[3x = 18\] -------(ii)

These give us the following solution: From equation (i) : \[y = 4.5\]

From equation (ii): \[x = 6\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Given the following figure, evaluate \[x\].
skill: Parallelograms and their Properties
feedback:
Using the figure, we can evaluate the value of \[x\] by applying any one of two properties of parallelograms.

By using the property opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal in length we get the equation: \[2x = 40\]. Solving this equation we get:
\[x = \frac{40}{2}\]
\[x = 20\]

We can use another property of parallelograms, 'consecutive angles of a parallelogram add up to \[180^\circ\]' to calculate \[x\] as well. This gives us:
\begin{align} 4x + 100 &= 180 \\ 4x &= 180 - 100 \\ 4x = 80 \\ x = 20 \\ \end{align}
Both properties give the correct value of \[x = 20\].

subject: Mathematics
q: In a parallelogram, the \[2\] pairs of opposite angles are _____.

feedback:
In parallelograms, the opposite angles are always equal which means they are congruent.
Only consecutive angles in a parallelogram are supplementary, not the opposite angles.
Remember that a parallelogram only has interior angles.

Only the diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other, not the opposite sides.

A trapezium is a quadrilateral that has only one pair of sides parallel to each other and a triangle has three sides.

feedback:
The correct order is ABCD < DCGF < BCEF < ADEF.

parallels, when their bases are in the same straight line and their sides opposite to these bases are also in a straight line.

subject: Mathematics
q: There are two parallelograms (ABCD and BCED) in the diagram between two parallel lines. Given BC = 4 and the distance between the two lines is 10. What is the area of the Quadrilateral ABFD?
skill: Prove that parallelograms on the same base between parallel lines are equal in area
feedback:
Given two lines are parallel, area of parallelograms ABCD and BCED are the same. We can show that
\[ area \: of \: triangle \: BCF = \frac{1}{4} \times area \: of \: BCED. \;\]

\[ Thus,\: area \: of \: triangle \: BCF = \frac{1}{4} \times \: area \: of \: ABCD. \;\]

As given that Base = BC = 4, and distance = altitude = 10, then

Area of quadrilateral ABCD = \[4 \times 10 = 40.\;\]

\[ Area \: of \: ABFD = \frac{3}{4} \times area \: of \: ABCD \: = \frac{3}{4} \times 40 = 30. \;\]

subject: Mathematics
q: If in parallelogram ABCD, E and F are the midpoints of AD and BC respectively, what can we say about the areas of ABEF and CDEF?
options: Area of ABEF > area of CDEF
Area of ABEF < area of CDEF
Area of ABEF = area of CDEF
Area of ABEF and CDEF are different, but there is not enough information to tell which is larger.
skill: Prove that parallelograms on equal bases and same altitude are equal in area
feedback:
We can show that ABEF and CDEF are also parallelograms.

As E and F are midpoint of AD and BC respectively, ABEF and CDEF have equal altitude by showing triangle AEG and triangle CFH are congruent.

Also, they have same base EF.

Thus, area of ABEF and CDEF are equal.

subject: Mathematics
q: ABCD is a parallelogram and BCFE is a square. If BE = 6 and G is the midpoint of DC, what is the area of ADG?
skill: Prove that parallelograms on equal bases and same altitude are equal in area
feedback:
We can show that area of triangle ADG = area of ABCD/4.
For ABCD and BCFE, they have equal base BC and equal altitude CF.
Thus, area of ABCD = area of BCFE = \[ 6 \times 6 = 36\;\] .
Area of triangle ADG is \[ \frac{36}{4} = 9 \;\]

Step 4: Label the point of intersection of the two perpendicular bisectors as \[I\] . The point I is the incenter of the triangle \[PQR\]  \[\longrightarrow\] diagram \[3\]

subject: Mathematics
q: The figure below describes the steps to construct a perpendicular to the line \[\overline{AB}\] from a point \[O\] outside the line. Given that the red, blue and orange arcs are centered at \[C, D\] and \[O\] respectively, which statement is correct?
options: The red and blue arcs must have the same radius.
The red and orange arcs must have the same radius.
The blue and orange arcs must have the same radius.
All the three arcs must have the same radius.
skill: Draw altitudes of a given triangle and verify their concurrency
feedback: Recall that only the red and blue arcs need to be of the same radius.

subject: Mathematics
q: The point at which the altitudes of a triangle meet is called the ____ of the triangle.
skill: Draw altitudes of a given triangle and verify their concurrency
feedback: The point at which the altitudes of a triangle meet is called the orthocenter of the triangle.

subject: Mathematics
q:
The diagrams describe the steps to locate the circum-center of a triangle \[ ABC\] in which \[ m\overline{AB}=5 \: cm\], \[m\overline{BC}=6 \: cm\] and \[m\overline{AC}=4 \: cm\].
What is a possible description for step \[3\]?
options: Draw a triangle in which \[ m\overline{AB} =5 \: cm\], \[m\overline{BC} =6 \: cm\] and \[m\overline{AC} =4 \: cm\].
skill: Draw perpendicular bisectors of a given triangle and verify their concurrency
feedback:
Observe that step \[3\] involves the construction of the red components in the above diagram.

Thus, the appropriate description is “Draw the perpendicular bisector of \[\overline{AC} \]”.

subject: Mathematics
q:
The figure below describes the steps to construct the perpendicular bisector of the line \[\overline{AB} \]. Given that the red and orange arcs are centered at \[ A\] and the blue and green arcs are centered at \[B\], which statements are correct?

Statement 1: The red and blue arcs must have the same radius.

Statement 2: The green and orange arcs must have the same radius.

Statement 3: All the arcs must have the same radius.
skill: Draw perpendicular bisectors of a given triangle and verify their concurrency
feedback: Recall that the red and blue arcs need to be of the same radius. While the green and orange arcs must have the same radius too, they can differ from that of the red and blue arcs.

subject: Mathematics
q: The point of intersection of the perpendicular bisectors of the sides of a triangle is called the ____ of the triangle.
skill: Draw perpendicular bisectors of a given triangle and verify their concurrency
feedback: The point of intersection of the perpendicular bisectors of the sides of a triangle is called the circum-center of the triangle.

subject: Mathematics
q: The figure shows the steps to locate the centroid of a triangle. What does the components in red describe?
options: It describes the steps to construct the median from \[P\] .
It describes the steps to construct the median from \[Q\] .
It describes the steps to construct the median from \[R\] .
It describes the steps to construct the altitude from \[Q\] .
skill: Draw medians of a given triangle and verify their concurrency
feedback:
Recall the steps to construct the median from \[Q\] . First, with \[P\] as center and radius greater than \[\frac{m\overline{PR}}{2}\] , we draw arcs one on each side of \[\overline{PR}\] .

Next, with \[R\] as center and the same radius, we draw two arcs that cut the previous arcs at \[X\] and\[ Y\] respectively.

We then join \[X\] with \[Y\] , cutting \[\overline{PR}\] at \[L\] . Lastly, we join \[Q\] with \[L\] , forming \[\overline{QL}\] which is the required median.

This is described by the components in red.

subject: Mathematics
q:
The figure below describes the steps to construct the perpendicular bisector of the line \[\overline{AB}\] .
Which statements are correct?



Statement 1: The red arcs are centered at \[A\] while the blue arcs are centered at \[B\] .

Statement 2: The red arcs are centered at \[B\] while the blue arcs are centered at \[A\] .
Statement 3: The radius of the arcs must be greater than \[\frac{m\overline{AB}}{2}\] .
Statement 4: The radius of the arcs must be less than \[\frac{m\overline{AB}}{2}\] .
skill: Draw medians of a given triangle and verify their concurrency
feedback: The curvature of the arcs tells us that the red arcs are centered at \[A\] while the blue arcs are centered at \[B\] . For the arcs to intersect, the radius of the arcs must be greater than \[\frac{m\overline{AB}}{2}\] .

subject: Mathematics
q: The point at which the medians of a triangle meet, is called the ____ of the triangle.
skill: Draw medians of a given triangle and verify their concurrency
feedback: The point at which the medians of a triangle meet is called the centroid of the triangle.

subject: Mathematics
q:
What kind of triangle is PDB if constructed through the following steps on a rectangle ABCD with AB > AD?
a. Join AC.
b. Through D draw DP//CA, meeting BA produced at P.
c. Join PD and BD.
d. Triangle PBD is constructed.
options: A non right angled isosceles triangle.
skill: Construct a triangle equal in area to a given quadrilateral
feedback: The triangle PDB must be a non-right-angled isosceles triangle.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Re-arrange the steps below into the correct order to construct a triangle equal in area to quadrilateral ABCD.
a. Join PC.
b. Join AC.
c. Through D draw DP//CA, meeting BA produced at P.
d. Then PBC is the required triangle.
skill: Construct a triangle equal in area to a given quadrilateral
feedback: The correct order should be bcad.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the given diagram, PD is the perpendicular bisector of BC and CDPQ is a rectangle. What is the relationship among of areas of ∆APO, ∆AQC and ∆BOD?
options: Area of ∆BOD = area of ∆APO + area of ∆AQC
Area of ∆BOD > area of ∆APO + area of ∆AQC
Area of ∆BOD < area of ∆APO + area of ∆AQC
skill: Construct a rectangle equal in area to a given triangle
feedback:
From the given information, area of rectangle PQDC is equal to the area of ∆ABC.
Thus, area of PQDC - area of AODC = area of ∆ABC - area of AODC.
Thus, area of ∆BOD = area of ∆APO + area of ∆AQC.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Construct quadrilateral DEFC from ∆ABC according to the steps given below.
a. From the mid-point of BC, D, draw DE which makes ∠CDE = 60°.
b. Draw AEF //BC cutting DE at point E.
c. EF = DC. Join C with point F
d. Quadrilateral DEFC is constructed.

Which of the following statements is correct about DEFC?
options: DEFC is a parallelogram equal in area to ∆ABC.
DEFC is a rectangle equal in area to ∆ABC.
DEFC is a rhombus equal in area to ∆ABC. (Rhombus is a shape with all four sides equal to each other)
skill: Construct a rectangle equal in area to a given triangle
feedback: DEFC is a parallelogram equal in area to ∆ABC.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Sarah learnt the lesson on how to draw a square of equal area to any given rectangle.



However, she wonders why the area of the square is equal to the rectangle. So she did some calculations. What should she try to prove?
skill: Construct a square equal in area to a given rectangle
feedback:
Area of rectangle = \[AD \times CD\;\]
Area of square = \[ DM^{2}\;\]

Thus, she should be attempting to prove \[AD \times CD = DM^{2}\;\]

subject: Mathematics
q:
ABCD is a rectangle and BC = 10 and BA =5. Given the following steps:
1. Extend AD to E making DE = CD
2. Bisect AE at O.
3. With center O and radius OA describe a semi-circle.
4. Extend CD to meet the semi-circle in M.
5. On DM as a side construct a square DFLM.
What shape is produced and what is its area?
skill: Construct a square equal in area to a given rectangle
feedback: The area of square produced has the same area as the rectangle. Thus its area is 50.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Given the following steps:
1.Draw a rectangle ABCD of area 42.
2.Extend BC to meet semi-circle at M.
3.Extend DC to E making CE = CB.
4.Take CM as a side to construct a square CMPN
5.Taking mid-point O of DC as center, OD as radius, draw semi-circle.

What is the correct order of steps to draw a square with area 42?
skill: Construct a square equal in area to a given rectangle
feedback: 1-3-5-2-4 is the correct order.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Refer to the figure below.

Follow the steps listed below and identify the type of angle for ∠CBM
1. With B as Center and radius =BM, where M is a point on AD, draw an arc which cuts AD at M.
3. Join BM and CM.
4. Then Triangle BCM is constructed.
options: It can be Acute, Obtuse or a Right angle.
skill: Construct a triangle of equal area on the base of given length
feedback:
Depending where point M is, ∠CBM can be right, acute or obtuse.
(When the arc cut the line AD at one point, ∠CBM will be right angle)

subject: Mathematics
q: ∆ABC, ∆ABD and ∆ABE are three triangles between two parallel lines. What can be concluded from these conditions?
skill: Construct a triangle of equal area on the base of given length
feedback: Since AB // CD the three triangles are equal in height. They share the same base AB so they are equal in width. With height and width equal, they are all equal in area.

subject: Mathematics
q:
How many elements are there in this matrix?
\[\begin{bmatrix}5 & 6 & 7 & 4\\5 & 1 & 3 & 5 \end{bmatrix}\;\]
skill: Able to define rows and columns of a matrix
feedback:
Each entry of a matrix is called an element of the matrix. Hence, there are \[8\] elements in this matrix.
\[4\] represents the number of columns while \[2\] represents the number of rows in this matrix.

subject: Mathematics
q: \[F= \begin{bmatrix}3\\ 7\end{bmatrix}\] is a _____ matrix.
skill: Able to define order of matrix
feedback:
As the given matrix has only one column, it is, therefore, a column matrix.

A row matrix contains a single row.

subject: Mathematics
q: \[X= \begin{bmatrix}3 & 7 & 5\end{bmatrix}\] is a _____ matrix.
skill: Able to define order of matrix
feedback:
As the given matrix has only one row, it is, therefore, a row matrix.

A column matrix contains a single row.

subject: Mathematics
q: If matrix \[A=\begin{bmatrix}6 & 7 & 4+3 \\ 3 &1 & 3+1 \end{bmatrix}\],\[B=\begin{bmatrix} 3 & 4 \end{bmatrix}\], \[C=\begin{bmatrix}2 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & 3 & 0 \\ 9 & 8 & 6\end{bmatrix}\], \[D= \begin{bmatrix}3+4 & 5 \end{bmatrix}\], and \[E=\begin{bmatrix}2 & 5 & 4+3 \\ 1 & 4-1 & 0 \\ 4+5 & 8 & 6\end{bmatrix}\], then which matrices are equal?
skill: Able to define equality of two matrices
feedback:
order of \[C = 3 \times 3\]
order of \[E = 3 \times 3\]
Hence, the order of \[C\] is equal to the order of \[E\]. The corresponding elements in both these matrices are also same:

\[C=\begin{bmatrix}2 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & 3 & 0 \\ 9 & 8 & 6\end{bmatrix}\]

\[E=\begin{bmatrix}2 & 5 & 4+3 \\ 1 & 4-1 & 0 \\ 4+5 & 8 & 6\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}2 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & 3 & 0 \\ 9 & 8 & 6\end{bmatrix} = C\]

Therefore, \[C = E\].

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[A= \begin{bmatrix}1 & 7\\2 & -5\\ 3 & 5\end{bmatrix}\], find \[A^{t}\].
skill: Negative and transpose of a matrix
feedback:
\[A= \begin{bmatrix}1 & 7\\2 & -5\\ 3 & 5\end{bmatrix}\]

To find the transpose of a matrix, we interchange its rows with its columns.

Therefore, \[A^{t} = \begin{bmatrix}1 & 2 & 3 \\ 7 & -5 & 5 \end{bmatrix}\].

subject: Mathematics
q: Which ONE of the following statements is correct about the transpose of a matrix?
options: A matrix obtained by interchanging the diagonal elements is called the transpose of that matrix.
A matrix obtained by interchanging the signs of diagonal elements is called the transpose of that matrix.
A matrix obtained by interchanging the rows into columns or the columns into rows is called the transpose of that matrix.
A matrix obtained by interchanging the signs and places of diagonal elements is called the transpose of that matrix.
skill: Negative and transpose of a matrix
feedback: The correct response is: A matrix obtained by interchanging the rows into columns or the columns into rows is called the transpose of that matrix.

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[P = \begin{bmatrix}3 & p \\ -9 & 2 \end{bmatrix}\], then find the value of \[p\] such that \[P^{t} = P\].
skill: Symmetric matrix and skew-symmetric matrices
feedback:
\[P= \begin{bmatrix}3 &p \\ -9&2 \end{bmatrix}\]

In order to find the transpose of a matrix, we interchange its rows with its columns.

Therefore,\[P^{t} = \begin{bmatrix}3 &-9 \\ p&2 \end{bmatrix}\]

It is given that \[P = P^{t}\]. Which means that:

\[\begin{bmatrix}3 &p \\ -9&2 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix}3 &-9 \\ p&2 \end{bmatrix}\]

In equal matrices, the corresponding entries are equal.
Therefore, \[p = -9\].

subject: Mathematics
q: Identify the diagonal matrix from the following matrices.
skill: Identity matrix, diagonal matrix and scalar matrix
feedback:
A diagonal matrix is a matrix in which at least one of main diagonal entries are non-zero and the rest of the entries, outside the main diagonal, are zero.

Therefore, \[\begin{bmatrix} -1& 0 &0\\ 0& 9& 0\\ 0& 0& 12 \end{bmatrix}\] is a diagonal matrix.

subject: Mathematics
q: \[ \begin{bmatrix}4 & 0\\ 0 & 4\end{bmatrix}\] is a _____ matrix.
skill: Identity matrix, diagonal matrix and scalar matrix
feedback:
A diagonal matrix is called a scalar matrix if all its main diagonal entries are the same and non-zero.

Therefore, \[ \begin{bmatrix}4 & 0\\ 0 & 4 \end{bmatrix}\] is a scalar matrix.
An identity matrix is a diagonal matrix with \[1\] as all its main diagonals entries.
A row matrix contains a single row, and in a rectangular matrix, the number of rows differ from the number of columns in it.

subject: Mathematics
q: If addition or subtraction is applied on two or more matrices, the order of the resultant matrix will _____.
options: be different from the order of the matrices
be the same as the order of the matrices
have different number of rows, but the same number of columns
have different number of columns, but the same number of rows
skill: Check for conformability for addition/ subtraction and add/subtract matrices
feedback: If addition or subtraction is applied on two or more matrices, the order of the resultant matrix will be the same as the order of the matrices.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let \[A= \begin{bmatrix}2& 7\\ -8& -6\end{bmatrix}\] and the real number \[-3\] be a scalar, then the scalar multiplication of matrix \[A\] with \[-3\] will result in the matrix _____.
skill: Multiply a matrix by a real number
feedback:
\begin{align}A &=\begin{bmatrix}2& 7\\ -8& -6\end{bmatrix}\\\\\mbox{Scalar multiplication }&= -3[A]\\\\&=-3\begin{bmatrix}2& 7\\ -8& -6\end{bmatrix}\\\\&=\begin{bmatrix}-3 \times 2 &-3 \times 7\\-3 \times -8 &-3 \times -6\end{bmatrix}\\\\&=\begin{bmatrix}-6& -21\\ 24& 18\end{bmatrix}\\\\\end{align}

Scalar multiplication of matrix \[A\] with \[-3\] results in the matrix \[\begin{bmatrix}-6& -21\\ 24& 18\end{bmatrix}\].
Scalar multiplication of a matrix leaves the order of the matrix unchanged.

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[A+(B+C)=\begin{bmatrix}-13 &12 & -4  \\ -8&-5 & -7\\ -3&11 & 19 \end{bmatrix}\], find \[-3[(A+B)+C]\].
skill: Commutative and associative law under addition
feedback: \begin{align}A+(B+C) &= \begin{bmatrix}-13 &12 & -4  \\ -8&-5 & -7\\ -3&11 & 19 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\\(A+B)+C &= A+ (B+C)  \mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{     (}  \because \mbox{associative law of addition of matrices)} \\\\\\\mbox{Therefore, }(A+B)+C &= \begin{bmatrix}-13 &12 & -4  \\ -8&-5 & -7\\ -3&11 & 19 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\\-3[(A+B)+C]&= -3 \begin{bmatrix}-13 &12 & -4  \\ -8&-5 & -7\\ -3&11 & 19 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\\-3[(A+B)+C]&= \begin{bmatrix}-3 \times -13 &-3 \times 12 &-3 \times -4  \\-3 \times -8&-3 \times -5 &-3 \times -7\\-3 \times -3&-3 \times 11 &-3 \times 19 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\\\mbox{Therefore, }-3[(A+B)+C]&= \begin{bmatrix}39 &-36 & 12  \\ 24 &15 & 21\\ 9&-33 & -57 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\\\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[(A+B)+C=\begin{bmatrix}-7 &6  \\ -2&9 \\ -3&16\end{bmatrix}\], find \[6[A+(B+C)]\].
skill: Commutative and associative law under addition
feedback: \begin{align}(A+B)+C&=\begin{bmatrix}-7 &6  \\ -2&9 \\ -3&16\end{bmatrix}\\\\\\(A+B)+C &= A+ (B+C)  \mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{ }\mbox{     (}  \because \mbox{associative law of addition of matrices)} \\\\\\\mbox{Therefore, }A+(B+C) &= \begin{bmatrix}-7 &6  \\ -2&9 \\ -3&16\end{bmatrix}\\\\\\6[A+(B+C)]&= 6 \begin{bmatrix}-7 &6  \\ -2&9 \\ -3&16\end{bmatrix}\\\\\\6[A+(B+C)]&= \begin{bmatrix}6 \times -7 &6 \times 6  \\6 \times -2&6 \times 9 \\6 \times -3&6 \times 16\end{bmatrix}\\\\\\\mbox{Therefore, }6[A+(B+C)]&= \begin{bmatrix}-42 &36 \\ -12 &54\\ -18&96\end{bmatrix}\\\\\\\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: What is the additive identity of the matrix \[\begin{bmatrix}-9 &1 &-5 \\2 &-1 &3\end{bmatrix}\]?
skill: Additive identity of a matrix
feedback:
Additive identity of a matrix is a matrix with the same order and \[0\] for all its entries.

Therefore, the additive identity of matrix \[\begin{bmatrix}-9 &1 &-5 \\2 &-1 &3\end{bmatrix}\] is \[\begin{bmatrix}0&0 &0 \\0 &0 &0 \end{bmatrix}\].

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[S= \begin{bmatrix}1&3&7 \\ 2&-1&-4 \end{bmatrix}\] then find the sum of matrix \[S\] and its additive inverse.
skill: Find additive inverse of a matrix
feedback:
\[S= \begin{bmatrix}1&3&7 \\ 2&-1&-4 \end{bmatrix}\]

            \[-S =\begin{bmatrix}-1&-3&-7 \\ -2&1&4 \end{bmatrix}\]        (additive inverse of matrix \[S\])

The sum of matrix \[S\] and its additive inverse (\[-S\]) will be

\[S + (-S) = \begin{bmatrix}1&3&7 \\ 2&-1&-4 \end{bmatrix} + \begin{bmatrix}-1&-3&-7 \\ -2&1&4 \end{bmatrix}\]

\[S + (-S) = \begin{bmatrix}1+ (-1)&3+ (-3)&7 + (-7) \\ 2 +(-2)&-1 + 1&-4 + 4 \end{bmatrix}\]

\[S + (-S) = \begin{bmatrix}0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \end{bmatrix}\]

Therefore, the sum of matrix \[S= \begin{bmatrix}1&3&7 \\ 2&-1&-4 \end{bmatrix}\] to its additive inverse is \[\begin{bmatrix}0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \end{bmatrix}\].

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[A\] is a matrix with an order of \[4 \times 2\] and \[B\] is a matrix with an order of \[2 \times 3\], then the order of the product of \[A\] and \[B\] is _____.
skill: Check for conformability for multiplication and multiply two (or three) matrices
feedback:
The order of a matrix resulting from multiplying two matrices follows this pattern: the number of rows of the first matrix and the number of columns from the second matrix.
Therefore, the order of the product of the matrices \[A\] and \[B\] is \[4 \times 3\].

subject: Mathematics
q: Find \[ \begin{bmatrix}1 &0 \\ 0& 1\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}1 & -4\\ -1 & 7\end{bmatrix}\].
skill: Check for conformability for multiplication and multiply two (or three) matrices
feedback:
First, check whether or not these matrices are conformable for multiplication.
Two matrices are conformable for multiplication if the number of columns in the first matrix are equal to the number of rows in the second matrix.

\begin{align}\mbox{Number of columns in matrix }\begin{bmatrix}1 &0 \\ 0& 1\end{bmatrix}&= 2\\\\\mbox{Number of rows in matrix }\begin{bmatrix}1 & -4\\ -1 & 7\end{bmatrix}&= 2 \\\\\end{align}

The number of columns in the first matrix are equal to the number of rows in the second matrix.

Therefore, the product \[\begin{bmatrix}1 &0 \\ 0& 1\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}1 & -4\\ -1 & 7\end{bmatrix}\] is possible.

\begin{align}\begin{bmatrix}1 &0 \\ 0& 1\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}1 & -4\\ -1 & 7\end{bmatrix}&= \begin{bmatrix} (1)(1) + (0)(-1) & (1)(-4) + (0)(7) \\(0)(1) + (1)(-1) & (0)(-4) + (1)(7)\end{bmatrix}\\\\&= \begin{bmatrix} 1 + 0 & -4 + 0 \\ 0 + -1 & 0 + 7\end{bmatrix}\\\\\begin{bmatrix}1 &0 \\ 0& 1\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}1 & -4\\ -1 & 7\end{bmatrix}&= \begin{bmatrix} 1 & -4 \\-1 & 7\end{bmatrix} \\\\\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: Under the associative law of multiplication of matrices if \[(AB)C=\begin{bmatrix} 8 \end{bmatrix}\], then \[A(BC)=\] _____.
skill: Associative law under multiplication
feedback: \begin{align}(AB)C&=\begin{bmatrix} 8 \end{bmatrix}\\\\(AB)C&=A(BC)                 ( \because \mbox{ associative law of multiplication of matrices})\\\\\mbox{Therefore, }A(BC)&=\begin{bmatrix} 8 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: The associative law under multiplication will be verified when \[(AB)C=\] _____.
skill: Associative law under multiplication
feedback: The associative law under multiplication will be verified when \[(AB)C= A(BC)\].

subject: Mathematics
q: Distributive law of multiplication over subtraction is written as _____.
skill: Distributive laws under multiplication over addition/subtraction
feedback:
According to the distributive law under multiplication over subtraction, for the matrices \[A\], \[B\], and \[C\], \[A(B-C) =AB -AC\].
\[AB - AC \ne AC - AB\] as subtraction is not commutative \[(A-B \ne B-A)\].
\[A(B+C)=AB+AC\] represents the distributive law under multiplication over addition.

subject: Mathematics
q: Left distributive law of multiplication over addition is written as _____ and right distributive law over addition is written as _____.
skill: Distributive laws under multiplication over addition/subtraction
feedback:
\begin{align}\mbox{Left distributive law of multiplication over addition } & = A(B+C)=AB+AC\\\mbox{Right distributive law of multiplication over addition } & = (A+B)C=AC+BC\\\\\end{align}
Meanwhile, \[A(B-C)=AB-AC\] and \[(A-B)C=AC-BC\] represents the left and right distributive law of multiplication over subtraction respectively.

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[A=\begin{bmatrix}0&1\\2&3 \end{bmatrix}\], \[B=\begin{bmatrix}1&0\\0&-2 \end{bmatrix}\], and AB=\[\begin{bmatrix}0&-2\\2&-6 \end{bmatrix}\], then find \[BA\].
skill: Commutative law under multiplication does not hold in general
feedback:
First, we check if these matrices are conformable for multiplication or not.
Two matrices are conformable for multiplication if the number of columns in the first matrix is equal to the number of rows in the second matrix.

\begin{align}B&=\begin{bmatrix}1&0\\0&-2 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\mbox{Number of columns in matrix } B&= 2\\\\A&=\begin{bmatrix}0&1\\2&3 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\mbox{Number of rows in matrix }A &= 2 \\\\\end{align}

The number of columns in matrix \[B\] is equal to the number of rows in matrix \[A\].

Therefore, the product \[BA\] is possible.

\begin{align}BA&= \begin{bmatrix}1&0\\0&-2 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix}0&1\\2&3 \end{bmatrix}\\\\BA&= \begin{bmatrix} 1\times 0+ 0\times 2 &1\times 1+0\times 3\\ 0\times 0+ (-2) \times 2& 0\times 1 +(-2) \times 3 \end{bmatrix}\\\\BA&= \begin{bmatrix} 0+0 & 1 + 0\\ 0 -4 & 0-6 \end{bmatrix}\\\\BA&= \begin{bmatrix} 0 & 1\\ -4 & -6 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: If matrix \[A\] is multiplied by its multiplicative identity \[B\], it gives \[AB=\begin{bmatrix} 2&4 & 5\\ 8&-3 & 4 \\ 1&2 & 3\end{bmatrix}\]. Using this information, find \[BA\].
skill: Multiplicative identity of a matrix
feedback:
The product of a matrix with its multiplicative identity matrix will be equal to the matrix itself.
\[AB = A= BA\].
Therefore, if \[AB= \begin{bmatrix} 2&4 & 5\\ 8&-3 & 4 \\ 1&2 & 3\end{bmatrix}\], then \[BA=\begin{bmatrix} 2&4 & 5\\ 8&-3 & 4 \\ 1&2 & 3\end{bmatrix}\].

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[A = \begin{bmatrix}7 & -6\\ 6 & -3\end{bmatrix}\], find \[ A^{-1} \times A\].
skill: Multiplicative identity of a matrix
feedback:
When a matrix is multiplied with its multiplicative inverse, the result is always an identity matrix of the same order.
Therefore, for \[A = \begin{bmatrix}7 & -6\\ 6 & -3\end{bmatrix}\], \[ A^{-1} \times A = \] identity matrix of order \[2 \times 2\].
Identity matrix of order \[2 \times 2 = \begin{bmatrix}1 & 0\\ 0& 1\end{bmatrix}\].

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[B = \begin{bmatrix} 1&3 \\ -2&0 \end{bmatrix}\], find \[B^t\].
skill: Verify the result (AB)^(t) = B^(t)A^(t)
feedback:
\[B = \begin{bmatrix} 1&3 \\ -2&0 \end{bmatrix}\]

The transpose of a matrix is obtained by interchanging the rows and columns of the original matrix.

Therefore, \[B^t = \begin{bmatrix} 1&-2 \\ 3&0 \end{bmatrix}\].

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[AB = \begin{bmatrix} 2&1 \\ 0&-1 \end{bmatrix}\], find \[(AB)^t\].
skill: Verify the result (AB)^(t) = B^(t)A^(t)
feedback:
\[AB = \begin{bmatrix} 2&1 \\ 0&-1 \end{bmatrix}\]

The transpose of a matrix is obtained by interchanging the rows and columns of the original matrix.

Therefore, \[(AB)^t = \begin{bmatrix} 2&0 \\ 1&-1 \end{bmatrix}\].

subject: Mathematics
q: Given that the determinant of the matrix \[\begin{bmatrix}4 & -5 \\1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}\] is equal to the determinant of the matrix \[\begin{bmatrix}5 & x \\3 & 8 \end{bmatrix}\]. What is the value of \[x\]?
skill: Find determinant of a 2-by-2 matrix
feedback: \begin{align}A&=\begin{bmatrix}4 & -5 \\1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}\\\\B&=\begin{bmatrix}5 & x \\3 & 8 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\mid A \mid &= \begin{vmatrix}4 & -5 \\1 & 2 \end{vmatrix}\\\\\mid A \mid &= (4)(2)-(-5)(1)\\\mid A \mid &=8+5\\\mid A \mid &=13\\\\\mid B \mid &=\begin{vmatrix}5 & x \\3 & 8 \end{vmatrix}\\\\\mid B \mid &= 5(8) -3x\\\mid B \mid &= 40-3x\\\\\mbox{As the determinants of matrices } A &\mbox{ and } B \mbox{ are equal, we have }\\40-3x&=13\\40-13&=3x\\27&=3x\\x&=9\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: Find the value of \[a\] when \[\mid A \mid =16\] for \[A =\begin{bmatrix} 2a & 7\\ 6 & 8 \end{bmatrix}\].
skill: Find determinant of a 2-by-2 matrix
feedback: \begin{align}\mid A \mid&=16\\\\\mid A \mid &=\begin{vmatrix}2a & 7\\6 & 8\end{vmatrix}\\\\\begin{vmatrix}2a & 7\\6 & 8\end{vmatrix}&=16\\\\(2a)(8) - (7)(6) &= 16\\\\16a - 42 &= 16\\\\16a &= 58\\\\a&= \frac{58}{16}\\\\a &= 3.625\\\\\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: What will be the value of \[a\], if \[B =\begin{bmatrix}2 & 4 \\2 & a+1 \end{bmatrix}\] is a singular matrix?
skill: Define singular and non-singular matrices
feedback:
The determinant of a singular matrix is \[0\].

\begin{align}B &=\begin{bmatrix}2 & 4 \\2 & a+1 \end{bmatrix}\\\\\mid B \mid &=\begin{vmatrix}2 & 4 \\2 & a+1 \end{vmatrix}\\\\\mid B \mid &=2(a+1) - 4(2)\\\\2(a+1) - 4(2)&=0\\2a+2-8&=0\\2a&=8-2\\2a&=6\\a&=3\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: If matrix \[A = \begin{bmatrix} 8 & x \\ -2& 1 \end{bmatrix}\] and det \[A=0\], then find the value of \[x \].
skill: Define singular and non-singular matrices
feedback: \begin{align}|A|&= \begin{vmatrix} 8 & x\\ -2 & 1\end{vmatrix}\\\\\begin{vmatrix} 8 & x\\ -2 & 1 \end{vmatrix} &= 0\\\\(8)(1) - (x)(-2) &= 0\\\\8 + 2x &= 0\\\\2x &=-8\\\\x&= \frac{-8}{2}\\\\x&=-4\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: If Matrix \[A = \begin{bmatrix} 2k+1&-2 \\ 2&3k-2\end{bmatrix}\] and \[A + Adj\] \[A = 0\], then find the value of \[k\].
skill: Define and find adjoint of a matrix
feedback:
\[A = \begin{bmatrix} 2k+1&-2 \\ 2&3k-2\end{bmatrix}\]

To find the adjoint of a matrix, the entries on the main diagonal are interchanged and the signs of the entries on the secondary diagonals are changed.

Hence, \[Adj\] \[A = \begin{bmatrix} 3k-2&2 \\-2&2k+1 \end{bmatrix}\]

\[A + Adj\] \[A = 0\]                    (\[\because\] given)

\[\begin{bmatrix} 2k+1&-2 \\ 2&3k-2\end{bmatrix} + \begin{bmatrix} 3k-2&2 \\-2&2k+1 \end{bmatrix} =0\]

We can find the value of \[k\] by adding the corresponding entries of these matrices.

Since these values add up to \[0\]:

\begin{align}2k +1 + 3k -2 &= 0\\5k -1 &=0\\5k&=1\\k &= \frac{1}{5}\\\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: Find the adjoint of \[\begin{bmatrix} -7&4 \\ 5&-3  \end{bmatrix}\].
skill: Define and find adjoint of a matrix
feedback:
Given matrix \[=\begin{bmatrix} -7&4 \\ 5&-3 \end{bmatrix}\]

To find the adjoint of a matrix, the entries on the main diagonal are interchanged and the signs of the entries on the secondary diagonals are changed.

Hence, \[Adj\] \[\begin{bmatrix} -7&4 \\ 5&-3 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} -3&-4 \\ -5&-7 \end{bmatrix}\].

subject: Mathematics
q: The adjoint of a square matrix is obtained by _____.
options: interchanging the main diagonal entries and changing the sign of entries on the secondary diagonal.
interchanging the main diagonal entries and changing the sign of all its entries.

changing the sign of all its entries.
skill: Define and find adjoint of a matrix
feedback: The adjoint of a square matrix is obtained by interchanging the main diagonal entries and changing the sign of the entries on the secondary diagonal.

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[A=\begin{bmatrix}4 & 8\\ 2 & 4\end{bmatrix}\], then \[A^{-1}\] _____.
skill: Find multiplicative inverse of a non-singular matrix A
feedback:
Multiplicative inverse of a singular matrix does not exist because its determinant is \[0\].
Therefore, we first check if the determinant of matrix \[B\] is non-zero.

\begin{align}A&=\begin{bmatrix}4 & 8\\ 2 & 4\end{bmatrix}\\\\\mid A \mid &= \begin{vmatrix} 4 & 8\\ 2 & 4 \end{vmatrix}\\\\\begin{vmatrix} 4 & 8\\ 2 & 4 \end{vmatrix} &= (4)(4) - (8)(2)\\\\\begin{vmatrix} 4 & 8\\ 2 & 4 \end{vmatrix} &= 16-16\\\\\begin{vmatrix} 4 & 8\\ 2 & 4 \end{vmatrix} &= 0\\\\\mid A \mid &= 0\\\\\end{align}

Since \[\mid A \mid = 0\], the multiplicative inverse of matrix \[A\] does not exist.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Use Cramer's rule to solve the following simultaneous equations:
\[3x + 3y = -9\]
\[3x - 4y = 47\]
skill: Solve a system of two linear equations using "Cramer's rule"
feedback:
Applying Cramer's rule to find the value of \[x\] and \[y\]:

\begin{align}\begin{bmatrix}3 & 3\\ 3&-4\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix} x\\y \end{bmatrix}&=\begin{bmatrix} -9\\47 \end{bmatrix}\\\\A&= \begin{bmatrix}3 & 3\\ 3&-4\end{bmatrix}\\\\A_{x}&=\begin{bmatrix}-9 & 3\\ 47&-4\end{bmatrix}\\\\A_{y}&=\begin{bmatrix}3 & -9\\ 3&47\end{bmatrix}\\\\\left | A \right |&=\begin{vmatrix}3 & 3\\ 3&-4\end{vmatrix}=(3)(-4) -(3)(3) = -21\\\\x &= \frac{|A_{x}|}{|A|}\\\\&=\frac{\begin{vmatrix}-9 & 3\\ 47&-4\end{vmatrix}}{-21}\\\\&=\frac{(-9)(-4) - 3(47)}{-21}\\\\&=\frac{36-141}{-21}\\\\&=\frac{-105}{-21}\\\\x&=5\\\\y &= \frac{|A_{y}|}{|A|}\\\\&=\frac{\begin{vmatrix}3 & -9\\ 3&47\end{vmatrix}}{-21}\\\\&=\frac{(3)(47) - (-9)(3)}{-21}\\\\&=\frac{141+27}{-21}\\\\&=\frac{168}{-21}\\\\y&=-8\\\\\end{align}

Hence \[\begin{bmatrix} x\\y \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 5\\-8 \end{bmatrix}\]

subject: Mathematics
q:
Find \[|A_y|\] from the following system of linear equations.
\[2x - 6y = 4\]
\[3x + 6y = 2\]
skill: Solve a system of two linear equations using "Cramer's rule"
feedback: \begin{align}\begin{bmatrix}2x &-6y\\ 3x&6y\end{bmatrix} &= \begin{bmatrix}4\\2\end{bmatrix}\\\\\begin{bmatrix}2&-6\\ 3&6\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\end{bmatrix} &= \begin{bmatrix}4\\2\end{bmatrix}\\\\|A_y|&=\begin{vmatrix}2 & 4\\3 & 2 \end{vmatrix}\\\\\end{align}
